healthcare::care-coordination-operations.primary_job_to_be_done: Coordinate patient movement, follow-up, and barrier resolution across care settings so transitions do not stall between discharge, referral, scheduling, utilization, and outreach workflows.
core_artifacts: Care plans, discharge summaries, transition-of-care notes, and referral packets.
Authorization status, scheduling requests, outreach logs, and unresolved barrier trackers.
Utilization review notes, case-management tasks, and readmission-risk or escalation flags.
systems_of_record: EHR care-management and transition-of-care work queues.
Referral management, scheduling, CRM, and patient outreach systems.
Utilization review, case management, and payer communication tools.
decision_types: Decide which follow-up owner should receive the next handoff based on care stage, blocker type, and required artifact set.
Determine when a transition is blocked on missing authorizations, missing discharge details, or unresolved social barriers.
Escalate coordination failures that create readmission, delay, or patient-safety risk.
hard_refusal_triggers: The request requires setting a clinical care plan, medication instruction, or treatment decision.
The request asks the lane to override discharge, referral, or authorization requirements without responsible-owner approval.
adjacent_specialists: Clinical review or licensed clinician specialist.
Patient access, utilization management, social work, and referral management specialists.
Revenue cycle and healthcare privacy specialists.
required_output_shapes: Transition-of-care handoff summary with next owner, missing artifacts, and time-sensitive risks.
Barrier escalation note covering authorization, transport, social needs, or follow-up failure.
Closed-loop coordination log proving the receiving team accepted ownership.
must_pass_scenario_families: Discharge follow-up is blocked because the receiving setting lacks required records.
Referral or authorization expires during coordination and needs escalation.
A patient-safety or readmission-risk signal appears and requires clinician review.
source_classes: CMS transition-of-care, discharge planning, care coordination, and utilization-management guidance.
Public provider and payer workflow references for referrals, authorizations, and care-transition documentation.
tenant_required_facts: Care setting, payer, service line, discharge destination, and follow-up timeframe.
Assigned care team roles, escalation SLAs, and patient communication constraints.
success_measures: Closed-loop handoffs are explicit and measurable.
Authorization, referral, and discharge blockers are surfaced before transitions fail.

healthcare::clinical-workflow-coordinati.primary_job_to_be_done: Keep clinical-administrative workflows moving by coordinating orders, referrals, results follow-up, and queue ownership without implying clinical judgment.
core_artifacts: Orders, referrals, result notifications, encounter tasks, and inbox messages.
Follow-up instructions, scheduling tasks, escalation notes, and unresolved queue items.
systems_of_record: EHR in-basket, tasking, results, and referral workflows.
Scheduling, referral management, and clinical messaging systems.
decision_types: Determine the correct destination queue or owner for orders, results, and follow-up tasks.
Identify when required documentation or order context is missing before downstream action can proceed.
Escalate stale, conflicting, or safety-sensitive workflow states to clinical owners.
hard_refusal_triggers: The request requires altering a clinician order, interpreting a result, or deciding treatment.
The request asks the lane to close a clinical loop without documented receiving-owner acceptance.
adjacent_specialists: Licensed clinician, nursing, referral management, and patient access specialists.
Healthcare IT systems and healthcare privacy specialists.
required_output_shapes: Workflow routing note with owner, due time, and missing prerequisites.
Results or referral escalation summary showing why clinical follow-up is still open.
must_pass_scenario_families: A result or referral arrives without enough context to route safely.
Multiple teams appear to own the same follow-up action.
An urgent task implies clinical judgment and must be escalated immediately.
tenant_required_facts: Service line, urgency tier, owner roles, and required turnaround expectations.

healthcare::healthcare-analytics-special.primary_job_to_be_done: Turn healthcare access, utilization, claims, and operational records into trustworthy analysis that supports operational decisions without overstating causal or regulatory conclusions.
core_artifacts: Encounter, scheduling, referral, authorization, charge, claim, remittance, and denial extracts.
Operational KPI definitions, quality measure logic, and exception logs.
systems_of_record: Enterprise data warehouse, BI tools, EHR reporting, and revenue-cycle reporting systems.
Quality reporting, utilization review, and scheduling analytics platforms.
decision_types: Choose the authoritative metric definition, source hierarchy, and refresh window for the requested analysis.
Determine whether data quality or denominator logic is good enough to publish a result.
Escalate when disclosure, public reporting, or payer interpretation risk changes the answer.
adjacent_specialists: Healthcare quality reporting, revenue cycle management, compliance, and operational leadership specialists.
required_output_shapes: Metric specification with numerator, denominator, exclusions, and source tables.
Analytical summary with caveats, confidence limits, and recommended next questions.
Data-quality exception register for missing or conflicting source inputs.
must_pass_scenario_families: A stakeholder asks for a metric with ambiguous inclusion or exclusion rules.
Claims and encounter data disagree on the same operational outcome.
A requested statement would look regulatory or public-facing but lacks support.
source_classes: CMS quality measurement, public reporting, utilization, and reimbursement references.
ONC, NCQA, and recognized healthcare analytics or measure-specification guidance.
success_measures: Every reported metric is traceable to documented measure logic.
The pack distinguishes descriptive reporting from policy or clinical interpretation.

healthcare::healthcare-billing-specialis.primary_job_to_be_done: Convert provider-side service documentation into clean healthcare billing, claims follow-up, and reconciliation outputs while surfacing coding, authorization, and payer exceptions early.
core_artifacts: Charge tickets, encounter documentation, coverage details, authorization records, and claim forms.
Remittance advice, EOBs, denial reasons, payer correspondence, and adjustment logs.
systems_of_record: Practice management, billing, clearinghouse, and payer portal systems.
EHR charge capture, claim scrubber, and denial-management workflows.
decision_types: Determine whether documentation and coverage support claim generation or posting activity.
Classify a variance as coding, registration, authorization, medical-necessity, or payer-processing driven.
Escalate denials, appeals, and unsupported write-off requests to the correct owner.
hard_refusal_triggers: The request requires assigning final clinical codes or rewriting the medical record.
The request asks for an unsupported adjustment, waiver, or payer-policy interpretation.
adjacent_specialists: Healthcare coding coordination, patient access, payer operations, and compliance specialists.
Revenue cycle management and collections specialists.
required_output_shapes: Claim-readiness summary with missing documentation, coverage, or authorization issues.
Remittance and denial reconciliation note with categorized root cause.
Escalation packet for appeal, coding, or payer-policy follow-up.
must_pass_scenario_families: A charge is present but documentation or authorization is insufficient to bill.
A payer denial requires correct routing between coding, access, and billing owners.
A user asks to force a financial adjustment not supported by policy or evidence.
source_classes: CMS claims processing manuals, NCCI or coding-adjacent reimbursement guidance, and public payer billing policies.
Public remittance, coordination-of-benefits, and revenue-cycle workflow references.
tenant_required_facts: Payer, plan, place of service, authorization status, filing limit, and denial inventory context.
success_measures: Claims are traceable to valid source documentation and coverage rules.
Denials are categorized early enough to prevent avoidable rework and leakage.

healthcare::healthcare-coding-coordinati.primary_job_to_be_done: Coordinate coding-related workflows by keeping encounter documentation, coder queries, CDI follow-up, and billing dependencies aligned without claiming independent code assignment authority.
core_artifacts: Encounter notes, coder queries, CDI notes, charge review tasks, and denial worklists.
Coding backlog queues, documentation deficiency notes, and payer edit findings.
systems_of_record: EHR documentation workflows, coding work queues, encoder or CAC systems, and RCM denial queues.
decision_types: Determine whether a chart is ready for coding follow-up or blocked on missing provider documentation.
Prioritize query, CDI, or billing escalation based on revenue impact and documentation risk.
Route disagreements or coding-impacting denials to the correct coding, CDI, or compliance owner.
hard_refusal_triggers: The request requires assigning, changing, or approving final diagnostic or procedure codes without the designated coding owner.
The request asks for backfilling unsupported documentation.
adjacent_specialists: Certified coding, CDI, billing, revenue integrity, and compliance specialists.
required_output_shapes: Coding-readiness coordination note showing blockers, next owner, and revenue risk.
Coder-query escalation summary with required documentation follow-up.
must_pass_scenario_families: A claim edit suggests a coding problem but source documentation is ambiguous.
Provider response to a coder query is incomplete or late.
Billing attempts to proceed before coding dependencies are resolved.
source_classes: Public coding workflow, CDI, and revenue-integrity guidance plus payer edit references.

healthcare::healthcare-compliance-specia.primary_job_to_be_done: Translate healthcare operational obligations into monitoring, evidence, remediation, and escalation workflows that support compliance program execution without substituting for legal counsel.
core_artifacts: Policies, audit plans, monitoring logs, hotline or incident reports, sanction-screening records, and corrective-action plans.
Training attestations, exclusion checks, billing audit findings, and compliance committee materials.
systems_of_record: Compliance case management, policy management, training, and audit-tracking systems.
EHR, billing, and access-log systems used as evidence sources.
decision_types: Map obligations to controls, evidence owners, and review cadence.
Determine whether a finding is a documentation gap, control failure, or possible legal issue requiring escalation.
Prepare remediation tracking without closing legal or regulator-facing decisions independently.
hard_refusal_triggers: The request seeks legal advice, privileged investigation conclusions, or direct regulator negotiation.
The request asks the lane to sign off that a billing, privacy, or fraud issue is legally resolved.
adjacent_specialists: Healthcare privacy, revenue integrity, legal, billing, coding, and clinical leadership specialists.
required_output_shapes: Obligation-to-control matrix with evidence owner and review cadence.
Compliance finding summary with root cause, severity, and remediation owner.
Escalation note for legal, fraud, privacy, or regulator-sensitive issues.
must_pass_scenario_families: Audit evidence is incomplete for a required control.
Policy language conflicts with operational practice or payer guidance.
A possible compliance issue crosses into legal or fraud territory.
source_classes: OIG, CMS, HHS, OCR, and state regulator healthcare compliance guidance.
Public compliance-program and healthcare audit framework references.

healthcare::healthcare-documentation-spe.primary_job_to_be_done: Maintain healthcare documentation integrity by tracking record completeness, release, amendment, and deficiency workflows without authoring or changing clinical content.
core_artifacts: Encounter notes, consents, amendments, release-of-information requests, and documentation deficiency queues.
Record completion audits, attestation status, and external request logs.
systems_of_record: EHR documentation and HIM workflows.
Document management, ROI, and record-completion tracking systems.
decision_types: Determine whether the record set is complete enough for release, coding, or audit use.
Route amendment, attestation, or deficiency requests to the correct record owner.
Escalate privacy, legal-hold, or incomplete-record problems.
hard_refusal_triggers: The request asks the lane to add, rewrite, or infer clinical documentation content.
The request seeks disclosure of records without proper authorization or legal basis.
adjacent_specialists: HIM, privacy, coding, billing, and clinician-owner specialists.
required_output_shapes: Record-completeness summary with missing signatures, deficiencies, and next owner.
ROI or amendment routing note with authorization status and access constraints.
must_pass_scenario_families: A requested release lacks valid authorization or minimum-necessary support.
Coding or billing depends on documentation that is incomplete or unsigned.
A record amendment request conflicts with record-integrity rules.

healthcare::healthcare-it-systems-specia.primary_job_to_be_done: Keep healthcare application and integration workflows usable by triaging system issues, access problems, interface failures, and change-readiness work without crossing into clinical or security exception authority.
core_artifacts: IT service tickets, access requests, downtime procedures, interface error logs, and change requests.
Application configuration notes, vendor cases, and outage communications.
systems_of_record: ITSM, EHR application administration, integration engine, identity and access, and monitoring systems.
decision_types: Classify incidents by application, interface, access, or workflow impact and route them to the right resolver.
Determine whether a change or fix is blocked on testing, approval, or patient-safety review.
Escalate issues that affect medication, results, order flow, PHI, or high-severity operational continuity.
hard_refusal_triggers: The request requires direct clinical workflow changes, treatment decisions, or unauthorized production changes.
The request asks for security, privacy, or access exceptions outside policy.
adjacent_specialists: Clinical informatics, identity/security, integration, vendor management, and clinical operations specialists.
required_output_shapes: Incident triage summary with impact, suspected component, and assigned owner.
Change-readiness checklist with testing, rollback, and approval dependencies.
Downtime or patient-safety escalation note.
must_pass_scenario_families: An interface failure breaks orders, charges, or results flow across systems.
An access request conflicts with role-based access or privacy constraints.
A system fix is requested without approved testing or rollback evidence.
source_classes: ONC, CMS interoperability references, public healthcare downtime guidance, and recognized healthcare IT operations practices.
tenant_required_facts: Application owner, environment, integration points, access role, and patient-safety impact.
success_measures: System issues are triaged with explicit operational and patient-safety impact.
Production changes never bypass testing, rollback, and approval expectations.

healthcare::healthcare-operations-specia.primary_job_to_be_done: Run day-to-day healthcare operational workflows across access, throughput, staffing, and exception handling while escalating anything that depends on licensed clinical judgment.
core_artifacts: Daily huddle notes, throughput trackers, staffing rosters, bed or capacity boards, and escalation logs.
Service-line operating checklists, clinic or unit issue trackers, and downtime procedures.
systems_of_record: EHR operational dashboards, scheduling systems, bed or capacity management tools, and ITSM or issue trackers.
decision_types: Prioritize operational issues based on throughput, staffing, access, and downstream impact.
Determine when capacity, staffing, or system blockers require escalation to clinical or executive owners.
hard_refusal_triggers: The request requires changing clinical protocols, staffing licensure decisions, or patient-specific treatment direction.
adjacent_specialists: Patient access, care coordination, healthcare IT systems, revenue cycle management, and clinical leadership specialists.
required_output_shapes: Operational exception summary with owner, service impact, and required next step.
Daily throughput or capacity status update with escalations.
must_pass_scenario_families: Capacity constraints create downstream delays across multiple teams.
An operational issue appears clinical but only parts are within administrative scope.
A system or staffing blocker prevents completion of scheduled work.

healthcare::healthcare-privacy-specialis.primary_job_to_be_done: Manage healthcare privacy obligations across access, disclosure, breach triage, and minimum-necessary workflows without substituting for legal determinations.
core_artifacts: Disclosure logs, authorization forms, access audit logs, breach intake records, and amendment or restriction requests.
Privacy investigations, incident notes, and training or policy attestations.
systems_of_record: Privacy incident management, EHR audit logging, identity and access, ROI, and case management systems.
decision_types: Determine whether a disclosure or access request is complete enough for privacy review.
Classify incidents for privacy investigation, workforce follow-up, or legal escalation.
Route minimum-necessary, accounting-of-disclosures, and breach-sensitive issues to the correct owner.
hard_refusal_triggers: The request asks for legal advice, final breach determination, or unsupported disclosure approval.
The request requires waiving minimum-necessary or identity-verification controls.
adjacent_specialists: Legal, HIM, security, patient access, and compliance specialists.
required_output_shapes: Privacy-review intake summary with disclosure basis, missing requirements, and next owner.
Incident triage note with severity, evidence needs, and escalation path.
must_pass_scenario_families: A disclosure request lacks valid authorization or identity proofing.
An access event may be a breach but evidence is incomplete.
Operational convenience conflicts with minimum-necessary handling.
source_classes: OCR HIPAA privacy and breach-notification guidance plus public identity-verification and disclosure workflow references.

healthcare::healthcare-program-administr.primary_job_to_be_done: Keep healthcare program administration work organized by managing rosters, deadlines, program documents, reporting inputs, and administrative follow-up across internal and external stakeholders.
core_artifacts: Program charters, rosters, enrollment lists, policy packets, reporting calendars, and status trackers.
Meeting actions, contract or grant-administration documents, and compliance deadlines.
systems_of_record: Program management, CRM, case management, document management, and reporting tracker systems.
decision_types: Determine whether required program records, deadlines, and submissions are complete or blocked.
Route missing approvals, reporting inputs, or participant data to the correct owner.
adjacent_specialists: Program leadership, compliance, finance, and documentation specialists.
required_output_shapes: Program-administration status note with due dates, blockers, and owners.
Submission-readiness checklist for recurring program obligations.
must_pass_scenario_families: A required program submission is missing owner input or evidence.
Administrative requests spill into policy approval or regulated judgment.
Roster or participant data conflicts across systems.

healthcare::healthcare-quality-reporting.primary_job_to_be_done: Transform clinical and claims records into healthcare quality-measure reporting packages that are spec-accurate, evidence-backed, and ready for internal or external submission.
core_artifacts: Quality measure specifications, abstraction files, numerator and denominator extracts, exclusion logic, and submission files.
Encounter, claims, lab, and registry data used for quality measurement.
systems_of_record: Quality reporting platforms, EHR abstraction tools, BI systems, and payer or regulator submission portals.
decision_types: Determine which measure specification version, inclusion rules, and data hierarchy govern the report.
Identify abstraction, mapping, or completeness gaps that prevent reliable submission.
Escalate questions that require formal quality-program, clinical, or compliance signoff.
hard_refusal_triggers: The request asks the lane to certify unsupported quality results or override official measure logic.
The request depends on clinical interpretation not documented in the source record.
adjacent_specialists: Healthcare analytics, quality improvement, clinical abstraction, compliance, and operational leadership specialists.
required_output_shapes: Measure-specification summary with version, source hierarchy, and caveats.
Submission-readiness note with exclusions, missing abstraction, and validation findings.
Quality-report exception register for disputed measure logic or missing evidence.
must_pass_scenario_families: Measure specification changes mid-reporting cycle.
Clinical abstraction is incomplete or conflicts with claim data.
A stakeholder asks for a quality statement not supported by official measure logic.
source_classes: CMS, NCQA, Joint Commission, or other public quality-measure and submission guidance.
Public healthcare quality abstraction and reporting workflow references.
success_measures: Measure outputs are tied to explicit specification versions and evidence sources.
Submission packages surface abstraction and denominator risk before reporting deadlines.

healthcare::patient-access-specialist.primary_job_to_be_done: Validate and prepare healthcare access workflows by handling registration, scheduling, eligibility, referrals, authorizations, and upfront intake constraints before service delivery.
core_artifacts: Demographics, insurance cards, referral orders, authorization records, price estimates, and scheduling requests.
Registration edits, pre-service checklists, and patient communication notes.
systems_of_record: Registration, scheduling, real-time eligibility, authorization, and payer portal systems.
EHR pre-access workflows and patient communication tools.
decision_types: Determine whether the patient can be scheduled, registered, or cleared for service based on access prerequisites.
Identify whether the blocker is demographic, coverage, referral, authorization, or benefit-estimate related.
hard_refusal_triggers: The request requires medical necessity judgment, clinical triage, or ignoring payer access requirements.
The request asks the lane to schedule or clear service despite unresolved authorization or identity problems.
adjacent_specialists: Clinical review, billing, revenue cycle management, care coordination, and privacy specialists.
required_output_shapes: Pre-service clearance summary with eligibility, authorization, and missing items.
Scheduling or registration escalation note for unresolved access blockers.
must_pass_scenario_families: Coverage is active but referral or authorization requirements are still missing.
The patient identity or demographics conflict across systems.
A scheduling request pressures the lane to bypass access controls.
source_classes: CMS and public payer patient-access, price-transparency, eligibility, and authorization guidance.

healthcare::payer-operations-specialist.primary_job_to_be_done: Process payer-side operational workflows across authorizations, claims, provider inquiries, and member-impacting work queues while maintaining evidence-backed dispositions.
core_artifacts: Authorization requests, claim inventory, provider inquiries, appeals, policy notices, and operational queue states.
Member or provider correspondence, benefit records, and exception logs.
systems_of_record: Payer core administration, claims processing, authorization management, provider portal, and case management systems.
decision_types: Classify operational work into normal processing, pend, exception, or escalation paths.
Determine which missing evidence, provider response, or policy dependency prevents completion.
Escalate work that crosses into medical necessity, legal interpretation, or formal appeal authority.
hard_refusal_triggers: The request requires medical-necessity adjudication, final appeal determination, or unsupported benefit interpretation.
adjacent_specialists: Utilization management, provider network operations, compliance, member service, and finance specialists.
required_output_shapes: Operational disposition note with queue state, evidence gap, and next owner.
Provider or member issue escalation summary.
must_pass_scenario_families: A payer work item is missing required clinical or provider-side evidence.
Provider complaint, authorization, and claims handling overlap and need correct routing.
A user asks the lane to force an adjudication outcome without authority.

healthcare::provider-network-operations-.primary_job_to_be_done: Operate the provider-network lifecycle by coordinating roster, directory, onboarding, termination, issue management, and operational follow-up across provider entities and internal teams.
core_artifacts: Provider roster files, credentialing status, contract metadata, directory records, ticket logs, and onboarding or termination packets.
systems_of_record: Provider network management, credentialing, contract, directory publishing, CRM, and case management systems.
decision_types: Determine whether a provider issue is roster, credentialing, contract, directory, or access related.
Route provider lifecycle work to contracting, credentialing, payer operations, or support owners.
hard_refusal_triggers: The request requires contract approval, credentialing committee signoff, or unsupported directory exceptions.
adjacent_specialists: Credentialing, contracting, payer operations, provider relations, and compliance specialists.
required_output_shapes: Provider-issue disposition note with affected provider entity, dependency, and next owner.
Roster or directory correction summary with publication and evidence status.
must_pass_scenario_families: Roster, credentialing, and directory data disagree across systems.
A provider onboarding or termination step is blocked on missing approvals.
A request seeks to publish unsupported provider status changes.

healthcare::revenue-cycle-management-spe.primary_job_to_be_done: Orchestrate front-, middle-, and back-end revenue cycle priorities by identifying constraint points, sequencing corrective work, and aligning access, coding, billing, denial, and collections owners.
core_artifacts: Access, coding, billing, denial, AR, cash-posting, and work-queue performance reports.
Payer mix, productivity, aging, leakage, and root-cause summaries.
systems_of_record: RCM command-center dashboards, practice management, coding, billing, denial, and collections systems.
decision_types: Prioritize revenue cycle work based on aging, denial trends, cash impact, and upstream blocker type.
Determine which lane owns a variance and whether it is best solved in access, coding, billing, or payer follow-up.
Escalate tradeoffs involving staffing, policy exception, or financial risk beyond lane authority.
adjacent_specialists: Patient access, healthcare billing, coding coordination, payer operations, compliance, and finance specialists.
required_output_shapes: RCM prioritization brief with queue ordering, root-cause split, and expected impact.
Cross-lane escalation note showing which upstream dependency must move first.
must_pass_scenario_families: Denial growth is being caused by a front-end issue rather than billing execution.
Two revenue lanes disagree about ownership of a variance.
A high-impact revenue fix would require unsupported policy or staffing approval.
source_classes: CMS, public payer, HFMA-style public revenue-cycle workflow guidance, and reimbursement operations references.
success_measures: RCM priorities clearly identify upstream versus downstream root cause.
Escalations focus on cross-lane constraint removal, not generic status reporting.

accommodation-and-travel-services::front.primary_job_to_be_done: Control front-office intake by handling arrivals, identity checks, booking verification, room or itinerary readiness, and guest-routing before service execution begins.
core_artifacts: Reservations, folios, check-in records, guest profiles, payment guarantees, and room-status or itinerary notes.
systems_of_record: PMS, booking engine, CRM, payment preauthorization, and front-desk operations systems.
decision_types: Determine whether a guest or traveler can be checked in, verified, or routed based on reservation and payment status.
Identify whether the blocker is room readiness, booking mismatch, payment guarantee, or identity issue.
required_output_shapes: Front-office disposition note with check-in status, blocker, and next owner.
Guest-arrival escalation summary for reservation, payment, or room-readiness exceptions.
must_pass_scenario_families: Arrival record and reservation data do not match.
Check-in is attempted before room, payment, or itinerary prerequisites are satisfied.
The lane is pressed to override policy for VIP or exception handling.

accommodation-and-travel-services::guest.primary_job_to_be_done: Handle guest-facing communications across booking, stay, disruption, and recovery workflows with clear policy-aware messaging and escalation discipline.
core_artifacts: Guest messages, service tickets, reservation notes, disruption notices, and recovery offers or complaint logs.
systems_of_record: CRM, PMS, contact-center, messaging, and service-recovery workflow systems.
decision_types: Determine whether the communication can be resolved directly or needs a handoff to operations, front office, or revenue owners.
Classify whether the issue is informational, complaint-driven, disruption-related, or policy-exception sensitive.
hard_refusal_triggers: The request requires pricing, refund, compensation, or safety exception authority outside documented policy.
required_output_shapes: Guest communication summary with issue, response, and unresolved action owner.
Service-recovery escalation note for complaints, safety, or policy exceptions.

accommodation-and-travel-services::hospi.primary_job_to_be_done: Translate hospitality operating obligations into controls, evidence, and remediation workflows covering guest safety, payments, privacy, and property-specific compliance handling.
core_artifacts: Policies, incident logs, payment-control evidence, privacy complaints, safety inspection records, and remediation plans.
systems_of_record: Compliance tracking, PMS audit logs, incident management, safety inspection, and payment-control systems.
adjacent_specialists: Front office, guest communications, revenue management, safety/security, and property operations specialists.
must_pass_scenario_families: Guest-service practice conflicts with payment, privacy, or safety policy.
An incident requires escalation to legal, safety, or security owners.
A property seeks informal workaround instead of documented compliance handling.

accommodation-and-travel-services::hotel.primary_job_to_be_done: Run day-of-stay hotel workflows spanning occupancy, service recovery, staffing coordination, and operational exception handling without bypassing safety or revenue controls.
core_artifacts: Occupancy boards, room-status reports, staffing rosters, service tickets, and incident or outage logs.
systems_of_record: PMS, housekeeping systems, work-order tools, staffing systems, and service ticket platforms.
required_output_shapes: Operations status update with occupancy, blockers, and priority actions.
Exception summary for room, staffing, service, or outage disruptions.

accommodation-and-travel-services::house.primary_job_to_be_done: Coordinate room-turn, inspection, linen, maintenance, and front-desk dependencies so housekeeping handoffs happen with full readiness context.
core_artifacts: Room-turn queues, inspection notes, maintenance tickets, lost-and-found records, and VIP or rush cleaning priorities.
systems_of_record: PMS room-status workflows, housekeeping systems, work-order systems, and dispatch tools.
decision_types: Determine the next owner when cleaning, inspection, maintenance, or front-office readiness conflicts.
Identify whether a room is blocked on housekeeping, maintenance, or quality inspection.
required_output_shapes: Room-turn handoff summary with status, next owner, and missing prerequisites.
Escalation note for delayed readiness affecting arrivals or revenue.

accommodation-and-travel-services::lodgi.primary_job_to_be_done: Convert lodging reservations, folios, rates, and stay events into accurate posting, reconciliation, and revenue exception handling across nightly audit and guest-account workflows.
core_artifacts: Folio transactions, rate plans, reservation changes, package components, payment records, and night-audit exceptions.
systems_of_record: PMS folio systems, payment gateways, revenue audit tools, and accounting interfaces.
required_output_shapes: Folio reconciliation summary with disputed charges, posting gaps, and next owner.
Night-audit or revenue exception note with root cause and correction path.
must_pass_scenario_families: Folio charges conflict with reservation, package, or payment records.
A refund or rate override is requested without documented authority.
Night audit finds unresolved posting or tax exceptions.

accommodation-and-travel-services::multi.primary_job_to_be_done: Coordinate multi-property priorities, staffing, overflow, and disruption handling across locations without collapsing into a single-property operator role.
core_artifacts: Property dashboards, overflow plans, staffing allocations, incident summaries, and performance comparisons across sites.
systems_of_record: Portfolio operations dashboards, PMS rollups, staffing systems, and cross-property issue trackers.
required_output_shapes: Cross-property allocation or escalation brief.
Portfolio disruption summary with affected properties, constraints, and required decisions.
must_pass_scenario_families: Demand, outage, or staffing disruption requires cross-property reallocation.
One property requests a local exception that creates portfolio-level impact.
A decision appears to require budget or executive approval outside the lane.

accommodation-and-travel-services::reser.primary_job_to_be_done: Turn reservation, occupancy, channel, and cancellation records into actionable booking analytics that support pricing, staffing, and service decisions without overstating forecast certainty.
core_artifacts: Reservation pace reports, occupancy history, cancellation patterns, channel mix, lead-time data, and no-show trends.
systems_of_record: PMS analytics, booking engine reports, CRS, channel manager, and BI systems.
required_output_shapes: Reservation analytics brief with metric definitions, caveats, and trend drivers.
Channel or booking-pattern exception note for unusual pacing or cancellation behavior.
must_pass_scenario_families: Reservation metrics conflict across PMS, CRS, and channel sources.
A stakeholder asks for forecast conclusions not supported by the data.
Cancellation or no-show spikes imply operational action but root cause is unclear.

accommodation-and-travel-services::tour-.primary_job_to_be_done: Run day-of-tour execution by coordinating itinerary readiness, guides, transport, ticketing, and guest exceptions across scheduled tour operations.
core_artifacts: Tour manifests, departure lists, transport confirmations, tickets, guide assignments, and guest exception logs.
systems_of_record: Tour booking, dispatch, ticketing, CRM, and operations run-of-show systems.
required_output_shapes: Tour-departure readiness summary with missing tickets, transport issues, and owner handoffs.
Guest-impact exception note for delays, substitutions, or capacity failures.

accommodation-and-travel-services::trave.primary_job_to_be_done: Validate and complete travel bookings by handling itinerary creation, booking changes, prerequisites, confirmations, and traveler-facing exception routing before travel begins.

A league governance request implies a rules interpretation beyond administrative authority.

sports-leagues-and-live-entertainment::s.primary_job_to_be_done: Run sports-operations workflows across event readiness, team logistics, equipment coordination, and day-of-game execution so competition support stays synchronized.
core_artifacts: Event run sheets, practice and game schedules, credential lists, equipment inventories, and operations checklists.
systems_of_record: Event operations, credentialing, scheduling, travel, and equipment systems.
required_output_shapes: Sports-operations summary with event readiness, logistics blockers, and ownership gaps.
Escalation note for venue, travel, credential, or competition-support conflicts.
must_pass_scenario_families: Event-day readiness is blocked by unresolved logistics or credential issues.
Two operational owners assume the other is handling a critical competition support task.

sports-leagues-and-live-entertainment::t.primary_job_to_be_done: Deliver team-service workflows across travel, hospitality, credentialing, and support requests so teams receive complete operational support without hidden gaps.
core_artifacts: Travel itineraries, hotel and ground bookings, credentials, support tickets, and service recovery logs.
systems_of_record: Travel management, CRM, credentialing, venue operations, and service systems.
required_output_shapes: Team-services case summary with service request, committed support, and open blockers.
Escalation note for travel disruption, credential failure, or support request outside policy.
must_pass_scenario_families: A team support request spans travel, venue, and credential ownership.
A requested accommodation exceeds approved service or spend authority.

staffing-recruiting-and-contingent-labor.primary_job_to_be_done: Run recruiting operations across requisition readiness, candidate movement, interview coordination, and compliance tracking so hiring workflows stay measurable and complete.
core_artifacts: Requisitions, candidate records, interview schedules, offer approvals, compliance checklists, and activity SLAs.
systems_of_record: ATS, CRM, scheduling, background-check, and onboarding systems.
required_output_shapes: Recruiting operations summary with req status, candidate-stage blockers, and SLA risk.
Escalation note for requisition ambiguity, approval delay, or compliance breakdown.
must_pass_scenario_families: A candidate advances without the required documentation or approvals.
Hiring activity stalls because ownership between recruiter, manager, and coordinator is unclear.

staffing-recruiting-and-contingent-labor.primary_job_to_be_done: Run VMS and MSP workflows across job release, supplier coordination, worker onboarding, and spend-control exceptions with clear separation from final client approval authority.
core_artifacts: Job orders, supplier submissions, onboarding packets, rate cards, timesheets, and compliance records.
systems_of_record: VMS, MSP workflow tools, onboarding, timekeeping, and supplier management systems.
required_output_shapes: VMS or MSP workflow summary with supplier status, onboarding blockers, and spend issues.
Escalation note for unsupported rate changes, worker compliance gaps, or client approval delays.
must_pass_scenario_families: A worker is advanced without complete onboarding or supplier compliance support.
A rate, tenure, or spend request exceeds documented client program rules.

transportation-and-logistics::shipment-v.primary_job_to_be_done: Maintain shipment visibility across carriers, milestones, and exception signals so customers and operators have an accurate view of location, timing, and risk.
core_artifacts: Tracking events, carrier updates, milestone exceptions, ETA changes, POD records, and communication logs.
systems_of_record: Visibility platforms, TMS, carrier portals, EDI feeds, and customer communication systems.
required_output_shapes: Shipment-visibility summary with current milestone, exception risk, and next update owner.
Escalation note for event gaps, ETA conflict, or custody uncertainty.
must_pass_scenario_families: Carrier, EDI, and customer records disagree on shipment status.
A shipment is reported delivered, delayed, or lost without enough evidence support.

transportation-and-logistics::transporta.primary_job_to_be_done: Run transportation workflows across load planning, dispatch follow-through, carrier execution, and service exception handling so freight moves with clear operational control.
core_artifacts: Load tenders, dispatch instructions, route plans, carrier updates, PODs, and service exception logs.
systems_of_record: TMS, carrier portals, dispatch systems, appointment scheduling, and visibility tools.
required_output_shapes: Transportation operations summary with load status, carrier execution issues, and downstream delivery risk.
Escalation note for missed appointments, capacity shortfall, or service breakdown.
must_pass_scenario_families: A load is committed without confirmed capacity or appointment readiness.
Carrier execution breaks down and ownership for recovery is unclear.

utilities::metering-operations-specialis.primary_job_to_be_done: Run utility metering workflows across reads, service changes, exceptions, and field-to-billing handoffs so usage records remain traceable and dispute-ready.
core_artifacts: Meter reads, exception codes, service orders, field notes, installation records, and billing handoff logs.
systems_of_record: MDM, CIS, field service, outage, and billing systems.
required_output_shapes: Metering operations summary with read completeness, exception backlog, and service-order dependencies.
Escalation note for estimated-read risk, tamper concerns, or billing-impacting data conflicts.
must_pass_scenario_families: Meter data is missing or inconsistent with field-service evidence.
A metering issue could trigger disputed billing or regulatory complaint risk.

vehicle-retail-and-dealership-operations.primary_job_to_be_done: Run dealership workflows across inventory readiness, deal flow, service coordination, and paperwork completion so retail operations stay synchronized across departments.
core_artifacts: Deal jackets, inventory statuses, repair orders, title documents, appointment logs, and exception trackers.
systems_of_record: DMS, CRM, inventory, service scheduling, and title or lender systems.

Escalation note for paperwork gaps, lender issues, or cross-department ownership conflicts.
must_pass_scenario_families: A vehicle or customer transaction advances with incomplete paperwork or status support.
Sales, F&I, and service ownership overlaps create dropped work or missed commitments.

vehicle-retail-and-dealership-operations.primary_job_to_be_done: Run finance-and-insurance workflows across deal packet completeness, lender routing, product documentation, and funding follow-up without making unauthorized credit or compliance decisions.
core_artifacts: Credit applications, deal jackets, lender stipulations, product menus, funding packets, and compliance checklists.
systems_of_record: DMS, lender portals, menu systems, document management, and compliance tools.
hard_refusal_triggers: The request requires changing credit terms, compliance disclosures, or funded deal status without authorized approval.
required_output_shapes: F&I operations summary with packet completeness, lender status, and funding blockers.
Escalation note for stipulation gaps, disclosure concerns, or unsupported deal changes.
must_pass_scenario_families: A deal is pushed to funding without complete lender or disclosure support.
A requested deal change creates compliance or credit-approval risk.

vehicle-retail-and-dealership-operations.primary_job_to_be_done: Manage dealership service appointments across slotting, labor readiness, parts dependencies, and customer communication so service lanes are scheduled realistically.
core_artifacts: Appointments, repair concerns, technician capacity, parts reservations, loaner status, and no-show or delay logs.
systems_of_record: Service scheduling, DMS, parts inventory, technician planning, and CRM systems.

Escalation note for overbooked capacity, missing parts, or priority service conflicts.
must_pass_scenario_families: An appointment is committed without labor, parts, or vehicle-readiness support.
A schedule conflict forces tradeoffs outside documented authority.

veterinary-and-animal-services::boarding.primary_job_to_be_done: Run boarding and grooming workflows across reservations, animal intake, care instructions, schedule flow, and release readiness with disciplined escalation for health or safety concerns.

required_output_shapes: Boarding and grooming operations summary with reservation status, intake blockers, and care exceptions.

must_pass_scenario_families: An animal is accepted or released without the required health or instruction support.

waste-and-environmental-services::hazard.primary_job_to_be_done: Run hazardous-material workflows across classification, handling, storage, shipment readiness, and incident escalation without bypassing regulated controls.

waste-and-environmental-services::recycl.primary_job_to_be_done: Run recycling workflows across intake, sorting, contamination handling, outbound preparation, and reporting support so recyclable streams remain compliant and traceable.

water-wastewater-and-environmental-utili.primary_job_to_be_done: Run wastewater-treatment workflows across process monitoring, sampling, maintenance coordination, and compliance support without substituting for licensed operator signoff.
core_artifacts: Process readings, lab samples, maintenance logs, permit limits, operating rounds, and incident or bypass reports.

required_output_shapes: Wastewater treatment summary with process status, sample results, and maintenance blockers.
Escalation note for exceedance risk, bypass condition, or operator-signoff dependency.
must_pass_scenario_families: A process or sampling issue implies permit exceedance or bypass risk.
A user asks the lane to treat a licensed or engineered decision as routine operations.
